title: Thirty-three new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta May 24
summary: On May 24, YKHC reports thirty-three new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta. New COVID-19 Cases The 33 are residents in: Aniak (1) Anvik (2) Bethel (3) Kalskag (2) Kipnuk (2) Kongiganak (1) Napaskiak (2) Scammon Bay (3) St. Mary’s (8) Tununak (9) — COVID-19 Updates and Community Information Updated C...

title: Kawerak Summer Childcare Program
summary: Are you interested in summer childcare for a child between 3 and 5 years of age? Kawerak UPLC child care program in Nome has a program from June 1 to August 10th! Our UPLC Child Care Program is open to the public and can be private pay or subsidy. Kawerak’s Child Care program may also be able to hel...

title: Arctic Alaska won’t solve high gas prices, but it can help the U.S. achieve its climate goals
summary: This piece originally appeared on The Well News . Cover photo: The Teshekpuk Lake wetlands are in the northeastern portion of the National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska, and while there may be oil beneath them, they are of supreme ecological importance to both avifauna and caribou. (© Kiliii Yuyan / www.kiliii.com) Lobbyists and lawmakers who have enjoyed full careers profiting from oil and gas have — predictably — blamed the Biden administration for current high prices and have called for a full-scale ramping up of domestic oil and gas leasing and the immediate approval of controversial projects in remote areas like the Arctic, all in the name of “energy security.” Sen. Tom C...

title: A Windfall of Support for Yup’ik Musician Qacung
summary: Yup’ik culture bearer and musician Qacung Blanchett received numerous notable recognitions in late 2021 and early 2022. The post A Windfall of Support for Yup’ik Musician Qacung appeared first on Alaska Magazine .

title: NANA Mourns the Passing of Alasuk Charlie Curtis
summary: The NANA Board of Directors is saddened by the loss of fellow board member, Alasuk Charlie Curtis, who passed away on May 10, 2022 at the age of 72. Alasuk served multiple terms on the NANA board totaling 19 years. “Alasuk had been a longtime champion of NANA. He had a tremendous vision of our share...

title: COVID-19 boosters now available for kids 5 to 11 years old
summary: YKHC began offering COVID-19 boosters to children 5 to 11 years old today, following CDC’s recent approval of booster doses for that age group. YKHC encourages parents to sign up their little ones for a booster to help protect them from severe COVID-19 illness. The CDC and YKHC also strengthened the...
